purpose: Five front-end initiated chemistry/materials research workflow demos that complement the existing 400-question benchmark.

principles.score_policy: Use public benchmark metrics or broadly accepted scientific ML / extraction / retrieval metrics; avoid subjective custom scores.
principles.agent_role: The agent is evaluated as a planner, tool caller, workflow organizer, and artifact generator; scientific value is judged by final task-native metrics.

public_metric_sources.name: d5sc08794e spectral-structure paper
public_metric_sources.use: Spectra-to-dihedral prediction is judged with MAE and correlation/Pearson r, with ablations or transfer tests used to support reliability.

public_metric_sources.name: d5sc09921h DIVE paper
public_metric_sources.use: Literature extraction is judged by accuracy, completeness, and precision/recall/F1 after matching extracted records to annotated records.

public_metric_sources.name: ScienceAgentBench
public_metric_sources.use: Workflow-level execution, generated artifact correctness, result correctness, latency/cost, and failure analysis.

public_metric_sources.name: Matbench
public_metric_sources.use: Materials property prediction with MAE for regression tasks and ROC-AUC for classification tasks under fixed folds.

public_metric_sources.name: MoleculeNet
public_metric_sources.use: Molecular property prediction using ROC-AUC or PR-AUC for classification and RMSE/MAE for regression.

public_metric_sources.name: DIVE / chemical information extraction literature
public_metric_sources.use: Field-level precision, recall, F1, numeric error, and completeness for literature/table/figure extraction.

public_metric_sources.name: ChemToolBench / CheMatAgent
public_metric_sources.use: Tool-selection precision/recall/F1, parameter-filling precision/recall/F1, and result F1 for chemistry/materials tool workflows.

demos.title: Unknown-material XRD phase identification from uploaded peaks and CIFs
frontend_input: Upload one observed XRD peak table as CSV.
Upload several candidate CIF files from a public crystallographic source or a frozen local benchmark set.
Ask DeerFlow to identify the best matching phase and generate a short evidence report.
agent_workflow: Inspect uploaded files and detect peak-table and CIF formats.
Run structure parsing and quality checks for each CIF.
Simulate theoretical powder XRD for each candidate.
Match observed and simulated peaks with a fixed two-theta tolerance.
Rank candidates and generate an XRD overlay / peak-match table.
State that the result is phase-triage evidence, not proof of phase purity.
why_it_shows_deerflow: Combines data recognition, materials structure tools, XRD simulation, ranking, plotting, and scientific caveats.
Produces auditable artifacts rather than a short answer.

demos.title: Materials property modeling on a Matbench-style task
frontend_input: Run five isolated front-end sessions, one per predefined Matbench fold.
For each session, upload only that fold's manifest, labeled training CSV, and unlabeled test CSV.
agent_workflow: Inspect the uploaded fold manifest and data schema.
Select and justify composition descriptors and a regression model.
Train only on the uploaded labeled training rows.
Predict every row in the uploaded unlabeled test fold.
Save predictions, run metadata, and a scientific report.
why_it_shows_deerflow: Demonstrates autonomous materials ML from uploaded data rather than memorized QA.
Exercises planning, code execution, materials featurization, modeling, and reproducibility controls.

demos.title: MoleculeNet molecular property prediction and error analysis
frontend_input: Upload a small held-out BBBP, HIV, ClinTox, Tox21, ESOL, or FreeSolv subset.
Ask DeerFlow to featurize molecules, train a baseline, evaluate, and explain common error cases.
agent_workflow: Validate SMILES and report invalid molecules.
Compute RDKit descriptors and/or Morgan fingerprints.
Use scaffold-aware or fixed split metadata when available.
Train a reproducible classifier or regressor.
Save probabilities/predictions and standard metrics.
Generate an error-analysis table with structures or descriptor summaries.
why_it_shows_deerflow: Shows chemistry-specific ML using RDKit, not only language reasoning.
Produces standard machine-learning outputs that reviewers can reproduce.

demos.title: IR/Raman spectra to molecular structure parameter regression
demos.public_task_family: Spectroscopy-to-structure regression from the azobenzene paper
frontend_input: Upload paired IR/Raman spectra and target dihedral angles.
Ask DeerFlow to build a regression workflow and identify predictive spectral regions.
agent_workflow: Inspect spectral matrix and target distribution.
Run preprocessing such as scaling, optional baseline correction, and train/test split.
Fit a regression baseline such as PLS, random forest, or ridge regression.
Generate held-out predictions and residual analysis.
Create a parity plot and spectral-feature importance summary.
why_it_shows_deerflow: Directly connects uploaded experimental/simulated spectra to molecular structure.
Highlights full scientific data analysis with standard regression metrics.

demos.title: Chemistry/materials paper extraction into a structured dataset
demos.public_task_family: Chemical/materials information extraction from articles, tables, and figures
frontend_input: Upload a paper PDF, supplementary Markdown, or a small batch of converted article snippets.
Ask DeerFlow to extract target fields such as compound, formula, property, unit, method, condition, and source location.
agent_workflow: Convert or read the document and identify sections, tables, captions, and units.
Extract records into JSON/CSV with source spans or page/table identifiers.
Normalize formulas, units, and numeric values.
Run chemistry/materials validation where applicable.
Optionally train a small downstream prediction model from the extracted table.
Generate an extraction audit report.
why_it_shows_deerflow: Demonstrates long-context document handling, extraction, normalization, validation, and dataset construction.
Connects directly to materials/chemistry literature-mining papers and uses standard information extraction metrics.
